title: Fog-bound ridge turnaround with delayed checkpoint evidence
route_context: Same-day out-and-back route with an exposed ridge section before the turnaround saddle.

timeline.label: T-180
timeline.summary: Team was on route but moving slower than the planned ridge pace.

timeline.label: T-120
timeline.summary: Fog reduced visibility and the remote status artifact showed stale position for one member.

timeline.label: T-60
timeline.summary: Route progress showed the team had not crossed the saddle before the planned turnaround time.

timeline.label: T-30
timeline.summary: Replay produced a local option set to turn around, shelter briefly, or continue to the saddle with stricter time bounds.

timeline.label: T-0
timeline.summary: Incident package marked overdue status when the team remained below the saddle after the planned exit window.

post_incident_evidence.summary: Leader reported that an earlier turnaround prompt would have made the delay easier to explain and coordinate.

baseline_summary: Baseline tracking noticed the overdue state near T-0 but did not summarize the stale remote status and fog together.
replay_summary: Phase 2 replay joined checkpoint delay, weather, and remote status references into a bounded local decision window.
known_outcome_summary: The team self-exited after waiting out fog; no rescue outcome is asserted.

assessment.rationale: Replay likely created a practical decision window before the overdue declaration, without asserting a certain final result.
